name: Bulk Certificate and Contract Generator with Carbone, Excel and OneDrive

parameters.content:
## Bulk Certificate and Contract Generator: Carbone PDF Batch with OneDrive Archive and Excel Status Update

### Weekly Scheduled Document Generation from Excel via Carbone and Microsoft 365

### How it works
This workflow runs every Monday at 08:00, reads pending rows from a Microsoft
Excel workbook, generates batches of PDF certificates and contracts using
Carbone's renderDocument operation, archives each file to OneDrive, notifies
a Teams channel with a summary Adaptive Card, and batch-updates the Excel
status rows to Completed via the Graph API.

### Setup steps
- [ ] Open the Config node and replace all placeholder IDs: workbookId,
      worksheetId, certificateTemplateFileId, contractTemplateFileId,
      certificatesFolderId, contractsFolderId, teamsTeamId, teamsChannelId.
- [ ] Connect your Microsoft Excel credential to the Read Pending Rows from
      Excel node.
- [ ] Connect your Microsoft Graph OAuth2 credential to the Fetch Header Row
      and Batch Update Excel via Graph API nodes.
- [ ] Connect your Microsoft SharePoint credential to both Fetch Template
      nodes and configure the file IDs from the Config node.
- [ ] Connect your Carbone credential to both Generate nodes. Ensure your
      Carbone templates use the field names output by the Build Carbone Batch
      Arrays node: certificates array uses name, email, company, role,
      courseTitle, completionDate, score, certificateId, issuedBy, issuerName.
      Contracts array uses name, email, company, role, startDate, contractRef,
      issuedBy, issuerName.
- [ ] Connect your Microsoft OneDrive credential to both Upload nodes and
      configure the target folder IDs from the Config node.
- [ ] Connect your Microsoft Teams credential to the Notify Teams Channel
      node and configure the target team and channel IDs from the Config node.
- [ ] Update issuedBy and issuerName in the Build Carbone Batch Arrays node
      to match your organization name and authorized signatory.

### Required credentials
- **Microsoft Excel** (row read)
- **Microsoft Graph OAuth2** (header row fetch + Excel batch status update)
- **Microsoft SharePoint** (template fetch)
- **Carbone** (PDF batch generation via renderDocument)
- **Microsoft OneDrive** (file archive)
- **Microsoft Teams** (completion notification)

### Required Excel columns
`Name` · `Email` · `Company` · `Role` · `DocumentType` · `CourseTitle` ·
`CompletionDate` · `Score` · `Status` · `ProcessedAt`

### Trigger · Config · Excel Read
Fires every Monday 08:00. All IDs loaded from Config. Header row fetched via Graph API to resolve column letters at runtime. Reads only Pending rows.

### Certificate Generation & Upload
Fetches template from SharePoint, generates batch PDF ZIP via Carbone, extracts files, uploads each to OneDrive, aggregates before continuing.

### Contract Generation & Upload
Fetches template from SharePoint, generates batch PDF ZIP via Carbone, extracts files, uploads each to OneDrive, aggregates before notification.

### Notify & Status Update
Builds Teams Adaptive Card with batch summary and posts to configured channel. Batch-updates Excel rows to Completed via Graph API using dynamically resolved column letters, chunked at 20 requests per call.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.notes: Replace all YOUR_* values before activating. workbookId and worksheetId: open the workbook in Excel Online — workbookId is after /items/, worksheetId is after /worksheets/. SharePoint file IDs: from the file URL, value between sourcedoc=%7B and %7D. OneDrive folder IDs: right-click folder → Details.

parameters.data: ={{ JSON.stringify({ d: { certificates: $('Build Carbone Batch Arrays').first().json.certificates } }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ d: { contracts: $('Build Carbone Batch Arrays').first().json.contracts } }) }}

nodes.name: Batch Update Excel via Graph API
